title: dryopteris lepigera
intro: Dryopteris lepigera, also known as the rustyback or rusty-leaved wood fern, is a species of fern native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Dryopteris lepigera fern can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
